title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 27 November 2024.

items.title: New **Cyberpunk** Netflix animation confirmed as 2077 tops 30 million sales
bullets: Live-action Cyberpunk project is still in the conceptual phase

items.title: **Star Wars Hunters** is coming to PC in 2025
bullets: A Steam playtest will be held from December 13-16, ahead of its Early Access release on January 27, 2025.

items.title: **CD Projekt Group** Q3 Results
bullets: Sales revenue: Down 49% year-on-year to €52.8 million ($55.2 million)
Net profit: Down 61% year-on-year to €18.1 million ($19 million)
Cyberpunk 2077 to date has "topped" 30 million copies while Phantom Liberty has sold eight million units.

items.title: **PlayStation** veteran **Shuhei Yoshida** to retire in January
bullets: The firm's development leader to step down after 31 years

items.title: **PS2** officially surpasses 160m sales, retaining its best-selling console crown
**Nintendo Switch** eShop and online services are ending in China in 2026
bullets: On March 31, 2026, the Chinese Nintendo eShop will stop selling games and any other software. After this date, all software, including paid and free titles, will no longer be available to purchase or claim. On May 15, 2026, players will no longer be able to download titles or redeem codes. DLC will also become unavailable at this time.
In China, the Nintendo Switch is sold in collaboration with Tencent, the Chinese tech giant that owns dozens of developers and other tech firms.

items.title: **Ubisoft** reportedly developing Animal Crossing-style social sim with Minecraft-like building
bullets: Former Gotham Knights creative director Patrick Redding said to be at the reins

Codename Alterra has been in the works for around 18 months. Alterra is said to have risen from the ashes of an earlier Ubisoft Montreal project, canceled after four years of development, with the two games supposedly sharing a focus on voxel creation.
